content.verdict: Anantara Maia is one of the most intimate resort experiences in the Seychelles — just 30 pool villas carved into a hillside above a sheltered cove on Mahé's southwest coast. Every villa comes with a dedicated butler, a private plunge pool, and direct beach access. The adults-only atmosphere (officially not restricted, but functionally couples-dominated) makes it feel genuinely secluded. This is the best choice on Mahé for honeymooners who want the butler-and-villa formula without the price tag of a private island. The spa is exceptional — one of the top three in Seychelles. The beach itself is small and calm, protected from trade winds. Best for: couples wanting maximum privacy on Mahé with butler service. Skip it if: you need a large beach, multiple restaurants, or a livelier resort atmosphere.
content.best_room: Book the Ocean Pool Villa — these villas sit directly above the water with a private infinity plunge pool and unobstructed Indian Ocean views. Request villa 24–30 for the most elevated position with the best sightlines and greatest distance from neighbouring villas. Avoid garden-view categories; the view differential at Maia is significant and worth the upgrade.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Butler Briefing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Mahé International Airport, transfer takes about 45 minutes to Maia's private cove. Your butler meets you at the villa — use this time to brief them on preferences for pillows, minibar, and dining timing. Order your first dinner in-villa on the terrace; jet lag makes a private sunset dinner the perfect low-key opener.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Maia Holistic Spa Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book the Maia Ritual couples treatment first thing — a 3-hour journey combining scrub, wrap, and massage in an open-air treehouse treatment room. Spend the afternoon floating between your plunge pool and the hotel's small but pristine beach. The water here is calm and clear with good snorkeling off the rocks at low tide.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day Trip to Anse Intendance
itinerary_7_nights.description: Ask your butler to arrange a driver to Anse Intendance, one of Mahé's wildest and most photogenic beaches, about 15 minutes south. Swim carefully (strong currents in season), but the scenery is extraordinary. Return for a private beach dinner arranged by your butler — Maia's chefs will set up a candlelit table on the sand.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkeling & Marine Life
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the resort's boat to the nearby marine reserve around Île Souris. The coral is healthy with hawksbill turtles frequently spotted. Return by noon and spend the afternoon in your villa pool with champagne delivered by your butler. Order the tuna carpaccio — the kitchen's Creole-French fusion is underrated.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Victoria & Creole Culture
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day excursion to Victoria, the world's smallest capital, with a stop at Sir Selwyn Clarke Market for fresh fruit and spices. Visit the botanical gardens to spot giant Aldabra tortoises. Back at the resort by 2pm for a leisurely poolside afternoon before a sunset cocktail at the clifftop bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Full Indulgence Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast in bed, then a second couples spa session — try the Hot Stone treatment. Afternoon is entirely unscheduled: your villa, your plunge pool, room service. Ask the butler to arrange a private sunset sailing trip around the cove. A final in-villa dinner under the stars caps the most relaxed day of the trip.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late checkout until noon if booked in the right rate. A slow breakfast on your terrace, one last swim in your plunge pool. Transfer back to Mahé airport. Allow 2 hours before departure — the mountain road can be slow at peak times.

honest_caveats: The beach at Maia is small — if you want a long stretch of white sand to walk, this is not the right hotel. It's about the villa and the intimacy, not the beach.
The hillside location means steep paths between villa, beach, and restaurant. If mobility is a concern, check the specific villa location before booking.
With only 30 villas, the spa books out fast in peak season (December–January, July–August) — reserve treatments before arrival.
Mahé is not the most photogenic island in Seychelles. If iconic granite boulder beaches are the priority, consider a night on La Digue or Praslin as an add-on.
content.hotel_email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon Stay — [Your Names] — Arrival [Check-in Date]

Dear Anantara Maia Reservations Team,

My partner and I are honeymooning at Maia from [check-in] to [check-out] (Booking reference: [reference number]).

We would be very grateful for the following:
1. An Ocean Pool Villa positioned for maximum privacy and ocean views — we would love villa 24 or higher if availability allows
2. Could the butler please stock the minibar with local Takamaka rum, fresh passion fruit juice, and sparkling water on arrival?
3. We would like to arrange a private in-villa dinner on our terrace on the evening of [date] — please let us know what the chef recommends that evening

This is our honeymoon and we are very much looking forward to the experience your property offers.

With warm regards,
[Your Names]

faqs.question: Is Anantara Maia Seychelles good for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Yes — it is one of the best choices on Mahé for honeymooners specifically. The 30-villa format, dedicated butler, and private plunge pools create genuine seclusion. The adults-dominant atmosphere and excellent spa seal the deal. Book an Ocean Pool Villa and brief your butler in advance for the best experience.

faqs.question: Best room for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: The Ocean Pool Villa is the definitive choice — private infinity plunge pool, unobstructed Indian Ocean views, and total seclusion. Request villas 24–30 for the highest elevation and the greatest distance from neighbouring villas. Avoid garden-view categories; the price difference buys a fundamentally different experience.

faqs.question: Best time to visit the Seychelles?
faqs.answer: April–May and October–November are the sweet spots — transition seasons between the trade winds bring calm seas, warm temperatures around 28°C, and lighter crowds. Maia's southwest-facing cove is sheltered year-round, but these shoulder months offer the best snorkeling visibility and the most pleasant sailing conditions.

faqs.question: Which island is Anantara Maia on and how do you get there?
faqs.answer: Maia is on Mahé, the main island of the Seychelles, so there is no inter-island transfer required. Fly into Seychelles International Airport (SEZ) — the hotel arranges a private car transfer of about 45 minutes to the resort's private cove on the southwest coast.

faqs.question: What's the snorkeling/diving like?
faqs.answer: The snorkeling directly off Maia's beach is good at low tide — granite boulders host good reef fish and occasional turtles. The hotel's boat can take you to the Île Souris marine reserve for better coral coverage. For serious diving, Mahé has several respected dive operators offering trips to sites like Shark Bank and Brissare Rocks.

faqs.question: Insider tip for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Email the butler team two weeks before arrival and ask them to arrange one surprise element — flowers, a handwritten note, or a bottle of Seychellois rum waiting in the villa. Maia's butlers are genuinely excellent at personalizing stays, but only if you give them the lead time. A 5-minute email pays dividends over 7 nights.
